subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Enter the Vocabulary Clue Lab and use sentence clues, word matches, and category shelves to solve word mysteries.
lesson.objective: I can use context, synonyms, antonyms, multiple meanings, and categories to understand words.
lesson.concept_explanation: Words can be understood by reading nearby clues, comparing meanings, noticing opposites, and grouping words that share attributes.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may choose a known meaning without checking context or may confuse synonyms with antonyms.
lesson.real_world_connection: Vocabulary clues help readers understand stories, informational texts, signs, and conversations.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the context, compare word meanings, choose the meaning that fits, and sort words by shared attributes.
lesson.mini_lesson: First read the sentence or word set. Then ask what clue, relationship, or category fits best. Last, check that the answer makes sense.

worked_examples.text: Watch: In The kitten was tiny, so it fit in my hand, tiny means very small.
worked_examples.sentence: The kitten was tiny, so it fit in my hand.

worked_examples.text: Demo: quick and fast are synonyms because they mean nearly the same thing.
Demo: apple, banana, and peach belong in the fruit category.

hint_ladder: Read the whole sentence before choosing a word meaning.

For multiple-meaning words, choose the meaning that fits the sentence.

misconception_bank.context_clue_ignored: Learner chooses a meaning without using the sentence clues.
misconception_bank.synonym_antonym_confusion: Learner mixes up same-meaning and opposite-meaning relationships.
misconception_bank.multiple_meaning_confusion: Learner uses a familiar meaning that does not fit the sentence.
misconception_bank.category_attribute_error: Learner sorts by a surface feature instead of the shared category or attribute.

game_theme.mission_intro: Use sentence clues and word relationships to unlock the Vocabulary Clue Lab.
